test.command: cd packages/api && npm test -- --maxWorkers=7 && cd ../frontend && npm test
test.note: API: 211 tests passed. Frontend: 412 tests passed. All 623 tests green.

lint.note: 15 warnings total (all pre-existing: max-lines-per-function, complexity). 0 errors.

blockingIssues.description: The pendingCurrentSideToken capture guard is always-true: handleInput() uses `if (pendingSelectedToken === null || ...)` but pendingSelectedToken is immediately reset to null inside the same block, making the condition always evaluate to true. Sequence for typing 'W','E','T','H': (1) first keystroke — pendingCurrentSideToken = USDC, store → null; (2) second keystroke — pendingCurrentSideToken = null (store already null from step 1); (3-4) same; (5) click dropdown — swap sees null → clear behavior instead of swap. The bug only manifests with multi-character typing; unit tests and browser test both fill the full value in a single input event, so neither caught this.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Change the capture guard from `if (pendingSelectedToken === null || ...)` to `if (pendingCurrentSideToken === null)` so only the first keystroke captures the pre-clear token. The store-clearing assignment remains unconditional. This is the 'capture-once guard' pattern now documented in .factory/library/frontend-component-patterns.md.

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Capture-once guard for pending state variables' section to .factory/library/frontend-component-patterns.md, documenting the anti-pattern (always-true guard overwrites on every keystroke) and the correct pattern (if (pendingFoo === null) { ... }).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note to the verification step for autocomplete/selection interaction features: unit tests for token selection flows should exercise multi-keystroke input (fire multiple input events with incremental values: 'W', then 'WE', then 'WET', then 'WETH') in addition to single-event fill. This catches the class of bug where a state variable is accidentally overwritten on subsequent keystrokes. Alternatively, use `fireEvent.input(input, { target: { value: 'W' } })` followed by `fireEvent.input(input, { target: { value: 'WE' } })` etc.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-token-swap-logic blocking bug was not caught by unit tests (single fireEvent.input with full value) or browser test (browser_type fills text at once, not char-by-char). The bug only manifests on second+ keystroke. Both fix features missed it because neither tested the incremental typing path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify browser verification requirement: when a feature's verificationSteps list does not include a browser step AND the feature is a pure store/logic fix with no UI surface changes, agent-browser verification may be skipped. Workers should not be penalized for skipping browser verification that was not called for in verificationSteps. Conversely, if the validation contract (fulfills field) references browser-observable assertions, agent-browser is required regardless of verificationSteps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-tokenlist-early-return worker correctly skipped agent-browser (feature verificationSteps had no browser step; fix was store-logic only). This matches the deviation from fix-default-tokenlist-always-present where the browser step WAS required by the validation contract (VAL-TKLIST-001) but the worker skipped it — those are different situations and the skill should distinguish them.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the asset-only exception: update 'Verify via browser/file inspection' to 'Verify via file/source inspection (browser inspection optional)'. The current wording implies browser inspection may be required, but for pure asset changes (favicon color tweak, CSS-only changes with no component logic) where verificationSteps are limited to grep/source inspection, file inspection alone is sufficient.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-favicon-dark-mode worker correctly skipped browser verification because verificationSteps were grep-only. The skill exception wording 'browser/file inspection' is ambiguous. The worker documented a deviation where none was needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: tokenListStore._ensureDefaultList should be made private since tests no longer call it directly
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking cleanup outside the scope of the fix feature; not a scrutiny concern
